title: Petrobras Launches Sustainable Aviation Fuel Produced Entirely in Brazil
content: The first deliveries of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) were announced by Petrobras in Rio de Janeiro. The company is the first to produce, entirely in Brazil, the fuel that receives sustainability certification according to ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization) rules. The volume of 3 thousand mÂ³ was sold to aviation fuel distributors operating at Tom Jobim International Airport (GaleÃ£o â€“ RJ). This volume corresponds to about one day of consumption at airports in the state of Rio de Janeiro. Sustainable aviation fuel, also known as SAF (Sustainable Aviation Fuel), can replace conventional aviation kerosene without the need for modifications to aircraft or fueling infrastructure. This makes it a practical and quick solution to reduce emissions from the airline industry. "SAF, produced by co-processing in Petrobras' refining park, is a solution that contributes to meeting the decarbonization goals of the airline sector. It is a competitive product, which meets strict international aviation standards. We are offering the domestic market the possibility of meeting global demands, anticipating compliance with CORSIA, which is an international program for the reduction of emissions from international flights", says the president of Petrobras, Magda Chambriard.
author: Analysts Account
